items.title: Iran war unlikely to trigger terror attacks in Indonesia: ex-militants
Forty Children On Bus In Ukraine Narrowly Escape Russian Drone Strike
items.description: A Russian drone struck close to a bus carrying 40 children in Ukraine’s Dnipropetrovsk region, as the children were heading to a resort in western Ukraine amid continued air attacks between Moscow and Kyiv.

items.title: Republicans ‘concerned’ after Trump threatens to withdraw more US troops from Germany
items.description: US announced withdrawal of 5,000 soldiers last week after German chancellor said US was being ‘humiliated’ by IranDonald Trump has threatened to withdraw more US troops from Germany after stunning European leaders and some senior members of his own party by last week announcing the withdrawal of 5,000 soldiers from Ger…

items.title: US to cut troops in Germany as Iran war spat deepens
items.description: US-German relations are straining as a spat deepens between President Trump and Chancellor Merz over the war on Iran.

items.title: Soldier seriously wounded during hazing ritual, 4 comrades indicted
items.description: IDF says abuse 'unacceptable' after violent initiation leaves combat soldier hospitalized with a punctured lung, in latest incident of extreme hazing The post Soldier seriously wounded during hazing ritual, 4 comrades indicted appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: 'Netanyahu convoca gabinetto sicurezza su possibile stop alla tregua in Iran e Gaza'
items.description: Lo riportano i media israeliani, riunione attesa per oggi

items.title: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal 'anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next
items.description: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.

items.title: 'I'm still the same on the inside, but I've changed on the outside': The reconstruction of George Ivanchenko, Ukrainian photographer and amputee
items.description: Wounded in a drone attack in the Donbas region of eastern Ukraine, young Ukrainian photographer George Ivanchenko captures intimate moments from his personal journey of recovery.

items.title: Ukraine battlefield: Advanced Ukrainian drones raise concerns among Russian forces
items.description: From Ukraine’s evolving battlefield, where drone warfare is redefining frontlines and so-called “killing zones,” to reports of “Martian drones” and their impact on both Russian and Ukrainian forces, we also look at the expanding use of unmanned systems in rescue operations, including the evacuation of a 77-year-old wom…

items.title: Putin’s Chornobyl: a Régime That Will Not Permit the Wound to Heal
items.description: From the 2022 Red Forest trenches to the St. Valentine’s 2025 drone strike, the Kremlin’s war is a war on the truth of 1986 – and the men who buried that truth are now being decorated for it.

items.title: How the Iran war is hurting travelers, airline industry
items.description: Europeans may have to reconsider their summer travel plans, as a jet fuel shortage triggered by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz could lead airlines to set higher ticket prices.

items.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
items.description: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

items.title: Russian State Prosecutors Seek Forfeiture of Jailed Billionaire’s Assets
items.description: Vadim Moshkovich is the first Forbes-listed billionaire to be arrested after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

items.title: Nuclear fears resurface among younger generations amid global tensions
items.description: The threat of nuclear Armageddon, a constant worry for many who grew up during the tense decades of the Cold War, is becoming a cause for concern for a growing number of young people.

items.title: Is our home safe? Is it rubble? For displaced Lebanese, anguished uncertainty.
items.description: Lebanese people displaced by war have been on an emotional roller coaster. A ceasefire has been extended, but fighting in the south hasn’t ended. Even as families yearning for home prepare to return, despite the uncertainty, they are warned not to take the risk.
